title: scanner uses Zip212Enforcement::GracePeriod unconditionally; should be On post-Canopy
description: scanner.rs try_decrypt_sapling always passes Zip212Enforcement::GracePeriod to try_sapling_compact_note_decryption. ZIP-212 enforcement should be GracePeriod only for blocks below the Canopy activation height (~1_046_400 mainnet), and On for all blocks at or above it. GracePeriod accepts pre-ZIP-212 (rcm-style) note plaintexts in post-Canopy blocks, meaning a note with a forged or misinterpreted rseed could be accepted by trial decryption and stored with rseed_after_zip212=false even though the block is post-Canopy. This corrupts the Rseed variant: the tx builder will reconstruct Rseed::BeforeZip212(fr) with bytes that are actually an AfterZip212 randomness seed, producing a wrong note commitment and a failing spend proof.
close_reason: Duplicate of nie-8wfc.13 which is already tracked
